$jsonSchema.title: adverse_events
$jsonSchema.description: Adverse event records reported during clinical trials. The most relationship-dense entity: every AE must reference a patient, a trial, and a specific intervention (all three mandatory). The AE does NOT store arm_label directly — to determine which arm an AE belongs to, look up the referenced intervention's arm_label. This indirect path (AE -> intervention -> arm_label) is essential for arm-level safety comparisons (AR-07).

ae_id.description: Unique adverse event identifier. Format: AE-XXXXXXXX.

patient_id.description: Reference to the patient who experienced this event. Mandatory — an AE without a patient is meaningless.

trial_id.description: Reference to the trial under which this event was reported. Mandatory.

intervention_id.description: Reference to the specific intervention being administered when the event occurred. Mandatory. Also serves as the indirect link to arm_label.

event_name.description: MedDRA-style preferred term. E.g. Neutropenia, Nausea, Elevated ALT.

system_organ_class.description: MedDRA System Organ Class category. E.g. Blood and lymphatic system disorders.

onset_date.description: Date the adverse event first occurred. Must be after patient enrolment.

resolution_date.description: Date the adverse event resolved. Null if the event is ongoing or was fatal.

outcome.description: Current outcome status of the adverse event.

serious.description: True if CTCAE grade >= 3 or the event required hospitalisation.

action_taken.description: Clinical action taken in response to the event.

causality.description: Investigator's assessment of whether the intervention caused the event.

lab_values.description: Laboratory data associated with the AE. Only present for lab-based events (e.g. low platelet count, elevated liver enzymes). Null for non-lab events like skin rash. Embedded because lab values are a 1:1 attribute tied to the specific AE occurrence.

test_name.description: Name of the lab test. E.g. ANC, ALT, Platelet count.

value.description: Measured lab value at time of event.

unit.description: Unit of measurement. E.g. x10^9/L, U/L.
